title: Tuned Global acquires London-based AI audio search firm Figaro.ai - Music Business Worldwide
text:
Tuned Global acquires London-based AI audio search firm Figaro.ai
October 8, 2025
By
Mandy Dalugdug
Pictured [L-R]: Con Raso and Lydia Gregory
B2B music streaming technology provider
Tuned Global
has acquired Figaro.ai, a London-based audio AI company developed by FeedForward
that specializes in music catalog search and discovery technology.
The deal marks Tuned Global’s latest AI-related acquisition, following the company’s
acquisition
of Swedish music technology company
Pacemaker
, including its AI-driven DJ applications, in 2023.
Pacemaker
powered
the launch of Tuned Global’s
Social Radio
in November 2024. The company
upgraded
Social Radio in June of this year with a real-time tipping feature.
The latest deal brings
Figaro
’s audio intelligence tech into
Tuned Global
‘s existing platform, which offers cloud-based infrastructure for businesses building their own streaming services and apps. Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
Figaro’s technology is said to enhance music catalogs’ searchability through automated tagging and content detection. The system is being used across sync, DSPs, UGC, and distribution.
Following the acquisition, Tuned Global stated that existing Figaro customers will continue to receive support, with access to expanded resources. The combined platform will operate as an open ecosystem, with Figaro functioning as a distinct component alongside other services and third-party partners.
Lydia Gregory
and
Kevin
Webster
, who founded Figaro, will join Tuned Global along with their team.
“With Figaro.ai joining the platform, we’re not only expanding that ecosystem with cutting-edge technology but also welcoming a highly skilled team whose expertise strengthens our ability to deliver music experiences that are powerful, flexible and future-ready.”
Con Raso, Tuned Global
Commenting on the acquisition of Figaro, Tuned Global CEO
Con Raso
said: “With Figaro.ai, Tuned Global cements its position as the most comprehensive music platform, where innovation across AI, fraud detection, rights management, search and recommendations can be built.”
Raso added:  “At Tuned Global, we see ourselves as the hub where innovation in music technology takes shape. We are building the largest open ecosystem of AI music intelligence, giving our clients maximum choice and real impact for the music industry.
“With Figaro.ai joining the platform, we’re not only expanding that ecosystem with cutting-edge technology but also welcoming a highly skilled team whose expertise strengthens our ability to deliver music experiences that are powerful, flexible and future-ready.”
“Being integrated into a platform of this scale means we can continue our mission with greater reach and impact, while ensuring continuity for the clients who already rely on us.”
Lydia Gregory, Figaro.ai
Figaro.ai
CEO and Co-founder Lydia Gregory, said: “Figaro.ai has always been about combining deep technical expertise with a passion for music discovery. I’m incredibly proud of the team that built this technology, and I’m thrilled that they are joining me as part of Tuned Global.
“Being integrated into a platform of this scale means we can continue our mission with greater reach and impact, while ensuring continuity for the clients who already rely on us. Together, we’re ready to help the industry deliver music experiences that are more relevant, premium, and engaging than ever before.”
Since its founding in 2011, Tuned Global has worked with more than 40 companies across 70 countries in sectors including telecommunications, fitness, media and aviation, it said. The company provides APIs, real-time analytics, licensing services, and customizable white-label apps.
Less than a month ago, Tuned Global
powered
the launch of
Sonsy
, a streaming platform designed specifically for the Mongolian market, and claimed to be the market’s “first world-class” DSP.
The company also partnered on the launch of the likes of
Plern
in Thailand,
Mjams
in the Pacific
Island
s,
Sewasew
in Ethiopia
, and Greenland’s
Tusass Music
, which all focus on local languages, cultures, and artists.
Recent partnerships were also struck with AI-powered analytics firm
Zetaris
, AI music startup
AudioShake
and
Beatdapp
.
